bibliographic.citation: Joseph Hall, ed., Selections from Early Middle English, 1130–1250, Part I: Texts (Oxford: Clarendon Press, 1920), vii, 222 pp.
bibliographic.title: Selections from Early Middle English, 1130–1250. Part I: Texts

entities.description: Hall's complete anthology of twenty-three numbered selections with a preface, manuscript table, source-following texts, corrections, and collated variants. Hall presents the arrangement as tentatively chronological and explicitly describes manuscript punctuation and intervention signs.
entities.label: Joseph Hall, Selections from Early Middle English, 1130–1250, Part I: Texts (1920)

rights.basis: Published in 1920; Hall died in 1927. The provider records also identify the volume as public domain in the United States.
rights.component: Hall's 1920 edited text, arrangement, preface, headings, and apparatus
rights.jurisdiction: United States and United Kingdom

workPortion.label: CME00035 portions in the complete Hall volume
notes: The selected portions are discontinuous; the cached complete scan retains preliminaries, Hall XIV–XV, the remainder of XX, blanks, and surrounding scan matter.
The scanned copy is a lithographic reprint from first-edition sheets whose title verso lists reprints through 1958, not the 1920 physical impression.

bibliographic.citation: Joseph Hall, Selections from Early Middle English, 1130–1250, Part II: Notes (Oxford: Clarendon Press, 1920), pp. 223–675.
bibliographic.title: Selections from Early Middle English, 1130–1250. Part II: Notes

entities.description: Hall's companion notes volume, with manuscript descriptions, phonology, accidence, dialect, metre, introductions, line commentary, bibliographies, glossary, and index for the texts in Part I.
entities.label: Joseph Hall, Selections from Early Middle English, 1130–1250, Part II: Notes (1920)

access.accessMethod: Contact the Cathedral Library to arrange scholarly consultation and ask whether complete-object photography can be supplied.

entities.description: A Worcester manuscript containing grammatical material and the Worcester fragments; Hall prints selected material from ff. 63r–64r. Current scholarship dates the relevant manuscript context to the first half of the thirteenth century, later than Hall's circa-1180 estimate.
entities.label: Worcester Cathedral Library MS F.174

physicalDescription.extent: at least 66 folios represented in the scholarly description
notes: Relevant selected fragments at ff. 63r–64r; adjacent glossary and further fragments remain part of the physical object.

access.accessMethod: Use the BL catalog record and digitisation service; request the complete current bound object, not only f. 85r. The same route applies separately to undigitized or legacy-unavailable Hall witnesses.
access.entityLabel: British Library Royal MS 5 F VII
notes: Individual current catalog records and dated availability findings for Vespasian A XXII, Nero A XIV, Titus D XVIII, Cleopatra C VI, Stowe 34, Otho C XIII, Royal 17 A XXVII, and Arundel 292 are recorded as evidence and open-question next steps.

entities.description: A twelfth- to thirteenth-century parchment volume; Saint Godric's hymns occur on an inserted leaf at f. 85r.
entities.label: British Library Royal MS 5 F VII

entities.description: The Peterborough Chronicle manuscript; Hall's selection III uses the final continuation on ff. 88v–91v. Current cataloging dates the main copying to 1121–31 and the final continuation to the mid-twelfth century.
entities.label: Bodleian Library MS Laud Misc. 636

physicalDescription.extent: 91 medieval folios plus preliminaries, interleaves, and binding surfaces
notes: The complete 302-canvas Digital Bodleian object includes boards, inside boards, spine, edges, flyleaves, explicit blanks, and adjacent contents.
physicalDescription.support: parchment with inserted paper and binding matter

entities.description: The charter cited by Hall for selection IV. Hall describes a French record hand using both insular and continental letter forms; an exact current item-record crosswalk was not verified.

holding.notes: Hall's designation retained pending modern catalog reconciliation.

entities.label: British Library Harley Charter III B 49

entities.description: A composite manuscript; Hall prints A Parable from the Middle English portion, marked across ff. 56v–58r in the edition.
entities.label: British Library Cotton MS Vespasian A XXII

notes: The current catalog describes a composite volume; the Middle English section begins before Hall's selected folios.

entities.description: A trilingual chronicle and poetic anthology used by Hall for The Proverbs of Alfred, Memento Mori, and one Owl and Nightingale text. Hall's legacy foliation is current foliation +73 for all three portions.
entities.label: Jesus College, Oxford MS 29

physicalDescription.extent: 257 folios in the current complete digital object, with boards and flyleaves
notes: Current f. 189r–192r = Hall 262r–265r; current f. 179v–180v = Hall 252v–253v; current f. 156r = Hall 229r.

access.accessMethod: The legacy LUNA result currently redirects to a Verify Access page; register or contact Lambeth Palace Library for complete-object access and reproduction terms.
access.format: Legacy page-image viewer or commissioned images

entities.description: A small West Midlands manuscript copied around the turn of the twelfth and thirteenth centuries. Hall uses Poema Morale on ff. 59v–65r and prose texts on ff. 15v–21v.
entities.label: Lambeth Palace Library MS 487

physicalDescription.extent: 68 folios (67 leaves in Hall's older description)

entities.description: A manuscript containing two copies of Poema Morale. Hall uses the second copy on ff. 69r–70v and the first copy at f. 12v to complement the Lambeth text.
entities.label: British Library Egerton MS 613

notes: The current complete IIIF object publishes 197 canvases from front through spine.

entities.description: A small thirteenth-century manuscript used by Hall for a Poema Morale copy on ff. 1r–8v and the Palm Sunday and Easter sermons on ff. 42v–46v.
entities.label: Trinity College Cambridge MS B.14.52

physicalDescription.extent: 90 folios plus covers, flyleaves, stubs, and ten published UV alternates

entities.description: A thirteenth-century Ancrene Wisse manuscript. Hall prints passages from ff. 56r–58r and 112v–117v and notes additions relative to the reconstructed original.
entities.label: Corpus Christi College Cambridge MS 402

physicalDescription.extent: 118 folios plus preliminary and postliminary leaves and binding views

access.accessMethod: Contact the College library about consultation and complete-object imaging; no anonymously accessible complete facsimile was verified.

entities.description: A late-thirteenth-century Ancrene Riwle manuscript. Hall prints selected Seven Deadly Sins material from ff. 116r–120v; his description notes 368 pages and extracts arranged differently from other copies.
entities.label: Gonville and Caius College MS 234/120

entities.description: A second-quarter-thirteenth-century manuscript containing Ancrene Wisse on ff. 1r–120v and The Orison of Our Lady on ff. 120v–123v. Hall uses it as the base of one Ancrene Wisse selection and for selection XVIII.
entities.label: British Library Cotton MS Nero A XIV

entities.description: A composite manuscript whose ff. 14–147 contain Ancrene Wisse and related works. Hall records unlettered Titus variants in selection IX and consultation for Sawles Warde.
entities.label: British Library Cotton MS Titus D XVIII

physicalDescription.extent: 148 folios in Hall's description; relevant component ff. 14–147

entities.description: A composite manuscript containing an Ancrene Wisse component on ff. 4r–199r. Hall cites this witness as C, supplying passages between asterisks and variants for selection IX.
entities.label: British Library Cotton MS Cleopatra C VI

physicalDescription.extent: 203 folios in the composite current record; Ancrene Wisse ff. 4r–199r

entities.description: An early-thirteenth-century manuscript written by three scribes and corrected by several hands. Hall selects Vices and Virtues passages from ff. 14v–16r and 47r–49r.
entities.label: British Library Stowe MS 34

entities.description: A composite manuscript containing one Brut version and an Owl and Nightingale copy. Hall prints Laȝamon from ff. 81r–84v and uses the Owl witness alongside Jesus 29; Laȝamon is omitted and only the first Jesus page of Owl survives in CME00035.
entities.label: British Library Cotton MS Caligula A IX

physicalDescription.extent: 261 folios plus early modern endleaves and unfoliated flyleaves; 543 canvases in the current complete composite IIIF object
notes: Hall's older description calls the Brut manuscript 192 folios while discussing that component.
Hall Laȝamon selection: ff. 81r, column 2–84v, column 2.
The Owl and the Nightingale: ff. 233r–246r.

entities.description: A fire-damaged manuscript preserving a parallel Brut version used by Hall for selection XIV, on ff. 65v–67v. The selection is absent from CME00035.
entities.label: British Library Cotton MS Otho C XIII

notes: Damaged in the 1731 Cotton Library fire.

entities.description: The Ormulum manuscript, commonly treated as substantially authorial/autograph. Hall prints selection XV from ff. 34v–35v; the whole selection is omitted from CME00035.
entities.label: Bodleian Library MS Junius 1

physicalDescription.extent: 118 folios plus covers, flyleaves, stubs, and exterior views
notes: Current scholarship dates the copying approximately 1170–1185; Hall dated it about 1210.

access.accessMethod: Request a catalog crosswalk and complete-object imaging from Bodleian Imaging Services, including binding and all leaves rather than only Hall's passages.

entities.description: An early-thirteenth-century devotional manuscript used for Sawles Warde through Hall line 339 and for a Saint Juliana parallel. Hall reports lost leaves after f. 80 and medieval associations with Ledbury, Godstow, and Much Cowarne.
entities.label: Bodleian Library MS Bodley 34

physicalDescription.extent: at least 80 folios; leaves lost after f. 80

entities.description: An early-thirteenth-century manuscript containing Sawles Warde, Saint Katherine, and Saint Juliana. Hall uses ff. 10r–10v, 11r–14r, and 56r–61v respectively.
entities.label: British Library Royal MS 17 A XXVII

entities.description: A late-thirteenth-century manuscript whose Middle English Bestiary begins at f. 4; Hall prints the work on ff. 4r–10v.
entities.label: British Library Arundel MS 292

entities.description: The unique manuscript of the Middle English Genesis and Exodus, dated about 1290–1310 in current aggregation. Hall selects Joseph and His Brethren from ff. 37v–49v.
entities.label: Corpus Christi College Cambridge MS 444

physicalDescription.extent: 81 folios plus preliminary and postliminary leaves and binding views

access.accessMethod: Request a current catalog record and complete-object imaging from Bodleian, including both bound components, bindings, blanks, and adjacent works.

entities.description: A composite manuscript; Hall's Kentish Sermons come from the later component beginning at f. 92 and occupy ff. 128v, column 1–133v, column 1.
entities.label: Bodleian Library MS Laud Misc. 471

physicalDescription.extent: at least 133 folios in two bound manuscript components

next_steps: Ask the Cathedral Library whether complete existing photography or microfilm exists.
If commissioning photography, request covers or boards, spine, pastedowns, endleaves, blanks, every leaf, adjacent works, and edge views if offered—not only ff. 63r–64r.
Record ff. 63r–64r separately as the Hall work portion with exact image locators and first/end deep links.
Confirm private retention, redistribution, scholarly publication, and commercial use separately in writing.
openQuestions.question: Can a complete color facsimile of the entire current bound Worcester Cathedral Library MS F.174 be obtained and retained locally?

next_steps: Ask BL Reference Services whether complete photography, legacy microfilm, or unindexed internal surrogates exist for each shelfmark.
Request each complete physical object or separately cataloged component, including covers, spine, pastedowns, endleaves, blanks, adjacent works, damaged fragments, and edge views if available.
For Harley Charter III B 49, first obtain the exact current catalog identifier and shelfmark crosswalk.
Keep private-retention permission distinct from redistribution, publication, and commercial licensing.
openQuestions.question: Can complete current-bound-object facsimiles be obtained for the Hall witnesses without verified public complete images, including Royal 5 F VII, Harley Charter III B 49, Vespasian A XXII, Nero A XIV, Titus D XVIII, Cleopatra C VI, Stowe 34, Otho C XIII, Royal 17 A XXVII, and Arundel 292?

next_steps: Resolve Lambeth's current Verify Access barrier and ask whether the two LUNA results cover the whole physical object or only selections.
Ask Caius for a modern catalog crosswalk and complete-object imaging route.
Ask Bodleian for exact catalog identifiers and complete existing or commissioned photography of Bodley 34 and Laud Misc. 471.
Request bindings, pastedowns, flyleaves, blanks, all leaves and adjacent works; document delivery and publication terms separately.
openQuestions.question: Can complete reusable facsimiles be verified or obtained for Lambeth Palace MS 487, Caius MS 234/120, Bodleian MS Bodley 34, and Bodleian MS Laud Misc. 471?

next_steps: Collate Hall pp. 61–75 and the separate notes volume against the three witnesses.
Record Nero's exact selected base run, every Cleopatra insertion between asterisks, and Titus/Cleopatra apparatus ranges.
Do not replace these roles with one undifferentiated Ancrene Wisse source relation.
openQuestions.question: What exact folio and line spans from Cotton Nero A XIV, Titus D XVIII, and Cleopatra C VI underlie every base, inserted, and variant reading in Hall selection IX?

next_steps: Request item-level production metadata and any downloadable source package from U-M.
Confirm whether the repeated REF 156 / p. 148 PB at the Saint Juliana–Owl shared-page division was intentional in U-M's production structure.
Keep TEMPHEAD scoped to 2016 validation and proofing rather than initial creation.
Do not infer OCR versus manual keying from the current XML alone.
openQuestions.question: Why were Hall XIV–XV and most of XX omitted, and what initial keying, source-image, correction, and reuse history precedes the documented 2016 CME review?

next_steps: Treat Bodleian, Trinity, and Parker CC BY-NC 4.0 bundles as private/noncommercial research copies unless the proposed reuse is plainly licensed.
Use Jesus College's CC BY 4.0 attribution and permalink requirements for reuse of Jesus 29 images.
Ask Parker and relevant libraries separately about high-resolution scholarly publication and any commercial use.
For British Library images, resolve UK status and manuscript-reuse terms before local bulk caching or publication.
openQuestions.question: What permissions are required for publication, redistribution, or commercial use of the cached noncommercial manuscript bundles and the uncached British Library viewer images?

reviewNotes: The primary cataloging subject is the discontinuous 21-piece electronic selection; Hall's complete 23-piece Part I is its source edition and a distinct entity.

Hall's manuscript table distinguishes direct text, parallel copies, inserted passages, variants, and consulted witnesses. Relations preserve those differences rather than labeling every manuscript an equal base text.

A complete cached IIIF bundle means every canvas in the provider's published manifest at review time. Trinity B.14.52 and the Parker objects omit some exterior views such as spine or edges, so their bundles are complete published inventories but not proven exhaustive photography of the physical objects.
Jesus College MS 29 uses a legacy foliation in Hall that is consistently 73 higher than the current Digital Bodleian labels: 229=156, 252=179, and 262=189. Both systems are recorded.

Internet Archive's generically titled selectionsfromea00halluoft PDF is Part II: Notes on direct title-page inspection. The complete Part I image source used here is p1selectionsfrom00halluoft, a lithographic reprint from first-edition sheets whose title verso lists reprints through 1958; neither layer is mislabeled as a scan of the 1920 Part I physical impression.
No British Library IIIF object was bulk-cached: current BL terms and the user's non-systematic-extraction constraint were treated conservatively even where complete public viewers exist.

lineage.summary: CME00035 is a U-M electronic selection from Joseph Hall's 1920 anthology, not one medieval work and not the complete printed volume. It preserves the title page and printed pp. 1–93, 117–148, and 176–222: selections I–XIII and XVI–XIX, only the first page of XX, and selections XXI–XXIII. Hall edited or collated these texts from twenty-three distinct surviving manuscript objects. This record separates repository XML, U-M encoding, Hall's scholarly edition, physical witnesses, and digital reproductions; records exact source roles and work portions; and caches only complete published provider objects where terms and practical retrieval allow. Complete-publication coverage is not conflated with complete physical-object photography, and private research retention is kept separate from redistribution, publication, and commercial permission.

catalogSummary.author: Various medieval writers, principally anonymous; Saint Godric and Henry II are associated with individual pieces
catalogSummary.date: Texts selected as examples of approximately 1130–1250; edition compiled and published 1920
catalogSummary.region: England; the pieces and witnesses represent multiple regional and dialectal settings rather than one place of composition
catalogSummary.title: Selections from Early Middle English, 1130–1250 [extracts]

catalogingSubject.label: Selections from Early Middle English, 1130–1250 [extracts]
catalogingSubject.scope_note: The cataloged unit is the U-M/General Corpus discontinuous electronic selection from Joseph Hall's 1920 Part I: Texts: the printed title page, selections I–XIII and XVI–XIX, the first printed page of XX, and selections XXI–XXIII. It is neither one medieval work nor the complete Hall volume; selections XIV (Laȝamon) and XV (Orm), most of XX (The Owl and the Nightingale), the preliminaries, and Hall's separate notes volume are outside this unit.

contentParts.label: I. Worcester Fragments
contentParts.summary: Three early Middle English verse fragments headed Sicut oves absque pastore, Homo putredo et filius hominis vermis, and Confabulabor cum amaritudine animae meae.

contentParts.label: II. Saint Godric's Hymns
contentParts.summary: Three short hymns traditionally associated with Godric of Finchale.

contentParts.label: III. The Peterborough Chronicle (A.D. 1132–1154)
contentParts.summary: Hall's selection from the final Peterborough continuation, organized under six annal headings.

contentParts.label: IV. Charter of Henry the Second

locators.value: Harley Charter III B 49; exact modern folio locator unresolved

contentParts.summary: A short royal charter selected as an example of early Middle English documentary prose.

contentParts.label: V. A Parable (Rex suos iudicans)
contentParts.summary: A Middle English religious parable with a Latin rubric.

contentParts.label: VI. The Proverbs of Alfred
contentParts.summary: Twenty-three proverb sections traditionally framed as Alfred's teaching; the attribution is not promoted here to known authorship.

contentParts.label: VII. Memento Mori
contentParts.summary: A fifty-line moral poem placed by Hall beside The Proverbs of Alfred because he thought it derivative.

contentParts.label: VIII. Poema Morale
contentParts.summary: Hall prints material from the Lambeth copy, two Egerton copies, and the Trinity copy rather than silently conflating them into one witness.

contentParts.label: IX. Ancrene Wisse

locators.value: base witness for Outer Rule selection; exact full run requires apparatus collation

contentParts.summary: Selected Seven Deadly Sins and Outer Rule passages printed from Corpus, Caius, and Cotton Nero, with readings from Cotton Titus and Cleopatra in Hall's apparatus.

contentParts.label: X. In Diebus Dominicis

locators.value: 15v–18v (Hall sequence begins 16r, returns to 15v, then continues)

contentParts.summary: A Middle English prose religious text from Lambeth Palace Library MS 487.

contentParts.label: XI. Hic Dicendum Est de Propheta
contentParts.summary: A Middle English prose religious text from Lambeth Palace Library MS 487.

contentParts.label: XII. Sermons for Palm Sunday and Easter Day
contentParts.summary: Two liturgical sermons, Dominica Palmarum and In die Pasche.

contentParts.label: XIII. Vices and Virtues
contentParts.summary: Two prose selections headed The Ark of Holy Church and Of Tears.

contentParts.label: XVI. Sawles Warde
contentParts.summary: Hall prints the text from Bodleian MS Bodley 34 through line 339 and supplies the ending from Royal MS 17 A XXVII.

contentParts.label: XVII. Saint Katherine
contentParts.summary: A short prose extract from the Middle English life of Saint Katherine.

contentParts.label: XVIII. The Orison of Our Lady
contentParts.summary: A 171-line Middle English Marian prayer poem.

contentParts.label: XIX. Saint Juliana

locators.value: 138–148; Royal and Bodleian versions alternate by page, with Royal ending on p. 148

contentParts.summary: Parallel prose extracts from Royal MS 17 A XXVII and Bodleian MS Bodley 34.

contentParts.label: XX. The Owl and the Nightingale (opening extract)
notes: The intellectual work is much longer than this electronic fragment.
The complete Digital Bodleian object establishes a consistent legacy/current foliation offset across all three Hall uses of Jesus 29: Hall 229=156, 252=179, and 262=189.
contentParts.summary: Only the twelve lines printed on Hall p. 148 are encoded; Hall's remaining pp. 149–175 and the Caligula parallel text are absent.

contentParts.label: XXI. The Bestiary
contentParts.summary: A complete selected Middle English bestiary treating animals and their moral significations.

contentParts.label: XXII. Genesis and Exodus: Joseph and His Brethren
contentParts.summary: A 590-line verse selection from the Middle English Genesis and Exodus, focused on Joseph and his brothers.

contentParts.label: XXIII. Kentish Sermons
contentParts.summary: Five prose sermons beginning with a sermon for Epiphany and continuing through Sexagesima.

assertion.notes: Hall signed the preface at Woodstock in January 1920; this dates the editorial compilation, not the medieval works.

notes: This is the publication date of Hall's edited anthology; the medieval pieces have distinct dates.

extent.description: The electronic selection has twenty-one BODY/DIV1 units (Hall numbers I–XIII, XVI–XXIII), 174 PB elements but 173 unique PB/@REF values, 110 P elements, 30 LG elements, and 2,717 L elements. REF 156 / printed p. 148 is represented twice: once at the end of the Royal Saint Juliana version and again at the start of The Owl and the Nightingale on that same page. It represents the title page and printed pp. 1–93, 117–148, and 176–222. The 173 legacy TIFFs match the 173 unique PB references exactly. Hall's complete Part I has twenty-three numbered selections and vii + 222 printed pages.

formStatements.description: Eleven encoded pieces are prose-only, nine verse-only, and Sawles Warde mixes prose with a short verse unit in the XML hierarchy.

languageStatements.label: Middle English
notes: The XML gives LANG=enm at text level; that broad encoding value should not erase the anthology's internal dialectal and chronological diversity.

scope.description: The selected medieval texts, including linguistic material near the Old/Middle English transition
Hall's titles, headings, apparatus, and editorial frame
Rubrics, quotations, and embedded phrases distributed across several selections

notes: Hall's title-date range is an editorial coverage claim, not a single composition date for all pieces and not a date for every surviving manuscript.
Hall's roman numbering is retained so the omissions of XIV and XV remain visible rather than being silently renumbered.
Exact physical-witness roles, digital-facsimile coverage, access routes, reuse constraints, and complete-object cache inventories are recorded in the paired lineage manifest.

next_steps: Consult current editions and manuscript scholarship work by work rather than projecting witness dates onto compositions.
Add dialect regions only where modern linguistic scholarship supports them.
Keep Hall's 1130–1250 phrase as an editorial title claim until the component review is complete.
openQuestions.question: What current item-level composition dates, localizations, authorship findings, and source-work relationships should replace or qualify Hall's tentative chronological ordering for all twenty-one selected units?

next_steps: Request item-level production and selection documentation from U-M.
Compare the legacy TIFF inventory and any predecessor source package.
Keep the omissions explicit in catalog and extent statements.
openQuestions.question: Why did the U-M encoding omit Hall selections XIV–XV and all but the first page of XX while retaining later selections?

next_steps: Ask U-M for item-level production metadata and source-package history.
Treat TEMPHEAD as 2016 validation and proofing evidence, not as the creation date.
Confirm from U-M production documentation that the repeated REF 156 / p. 148 PB at the Saint Juliana–Owl division was an intentional shared-page boundary rather than a later structural workaround.
openQuestions.question: What initial keying method, source-image provenance, correction history, and downloadable-source terms apply to U-M CME00035 before its documented 2016 review?

assertion.notes: The preface is signed at Woodstock; it supports editorial association but not continuous residence or the origins of the medieval pieces.
Publication place of the Clarendon Press edition.

scope.description: Hall's arrangement of distinct medieval pieces as a chronological teaching anthology
Manuscript collation and consultation varying by numbered selection

assertion.notes: Some pieces contain traditional, translated, or adapted material; this collective statement records anonymous textual responsibility without collapsing those histories.
The responsibility is scoped to selection II and preserves the traditional attribution.
The schema lacks an issuer role; Henry is recorded as issuer in the scope and not identified as the drafting clerk, verbal composer, or scribe.

scope.description: Royal issuer of the documentary act

subjects.label: Early Middle English language and literature
Christian doctrine, devotion, and moral instruction

scope.description: A dominant but not exclusive subject across the religious pieces

summaries.value: Twenty-one numbered units preserve verse fragments, hymns, a chronicle, charter, parable, proverbs, moral and devotional works, sermons, saints' lives, a one-page opening from The Owl and the Nightingale, a bestiary, biblical paraphrase, and Kentish sermons. The inherited numbering jumps from XIII to XVI because Hall's Laȝamon and Orm selections are omitted.

The checked-in XML represents printed pp. 1–93, 117–148, and 176–222 plus the title page. It omits preliminaries, pp. 94–116 (Hall XIV–XV), pp. 149–175 (most of XX), and all of Hall's Part II notes. Metadata for the electronic selection is therefore separated from the complete edition and its twenty-three manuscript-source groupings.

assertion.notes: The bracketed qualifier follows the U-M catalog and distinguishes the electronic selection from Hall's complete volume.
This title belongs to Hall's complete Part I, not to a single medieval composition.

scope.description: Title of the 1920 source edition from which CME00035 selects

titles.value: Selections from Early Middle English, 1130–1250. Part I: Texts

work.author: Various medieval writers, principally anonymous; Saint Godric and Henry II are associated with individual pieces
work.dateDisplay: Texts selected as examples of approximately 1130–1250; edition compiled and published 1920
work.regionDisplay: England; the pieces and witnesses represent multiple regional and dialectal settings rather than one place of composition
